mcqs.question: What is the block size of the Data Encryption Standard (DES)?
mcqs.explanation: The Data Encryption Standard (DES) operates on 64-bit blocks of plaintext.

mcqs.question: What is the key size of the Data Encryption Standard (DES)?
mcqs.explanation: The Data Encryption Standard (DES) uses a 56-bit key.

mcqs.question: What is the main purpose of the initial permutation (IP) in DES?

options.text: To rearrange the bits of the plaintext block
To substitute the bits of the plaintext block

mcqs.explanation: The initial permutation (IP) rearranges the bits of the plaintext block.

mcqs.question: What is the function of the substitution boxes (S-Boxes) in DES?

options.text: To permute the bits of the data

mcqs.explanation: The substitution boxes (S-Boxes) substitute 6-bit blocks of data.

mcqs.question: How many rounds of substitution and permutation are used in DES?
mcqs.explanation: The Data Encryption Standard (DES) uses 16 rounds of substitution and permutation.

mcqs.question: Why is DES no longer considered secure for modern applications?

options.text: Because of its small block size
Because of its small key size
Because of its slow encryption speed
Because of its complex encryption algorithm

mcqs.explanation: DES is no longer considered secure because of its small key size, which makes it vulnerable to brute-force attacks.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the final permutation (FP) in DES?

options.text: To rearrange the bits of the ciphertext block
To substitute the bits of the ciphertext block

mcqs.explanation: The final permutation (FP) rearranges the bits of the ciphertext block.

mcqs.question: Why is DES still studied in cryptography courses despite its limitations?
mcqs.explanation: DES is still studied in cryptography courses because of its educational value, as it provides a simple and well-understood example of a block cipher.
